info.description: In this section are described the interfaces exposed by ASPSP to manage bulk payment and subresource transactions
info.title: paymentServices

parameters.description: This field indicates the authorization purpose.The allowed values are:AUTHCANC
parameters.name: authorization-purpose

parameters.description: The identification of the PSU in the ASPSP client interface.
parameters.name: psu-id

parameters.description: Type of the PSU-ID, needed in scenarios where PSUs have several PSU-IDs as access possibility.
parameters.name: psu-id-type

parameters.description: Identification of a Corporate, only used in a corporate context
parameters.name: psu-corporate-id

parameters.description: This is describing the type of the identification needed by the ASPSP to identify the PSU-Corporate-ID content.
parameters.name: psu-corporate-id-type

parameters.description: A unique registration number for TPP identification
parameters.name: tpp-registration-number

parameters.description: The TPP name
parameters.name: tpp-name

parameters.description: URI of the TPP, where the transaction flow shall be redirected to after a Redirect. Mandatory for redirect or OAuth SCA Approach
parameters.name: tpp-redirect-uri

parameters.description: If this URI is contained, the TPP is asking to redirect the transaction flow to this address instead of the TPP-Redirect-URI in case of a negative result of the redirect SCA method. This might be ignored by the ASPSP.
parameters.name: tpp-nok-redirect-uri

parameters.description: The identification of the ASPSP (for example ABI)
parameters.name: aspsp-code

parameters.description: Identifier of the product in ASPSP scope.
parameters.name: aspsp-product-code

parameters.description: UUID of the request, unique to the call, as determined by the initiating party.
parameters.name: request-id

parameters.description: The value list of products supported is:- sepa-credit-transfers - instant-sepa-credit-transfers - target-2-payments - cross-border-credit-transfers- pain.001-sepa-credit-transfers - pain.001-instant-sepa-credit-transfers - pain.001-target-2-payments - pain.001-cross-border-credit-transfers
parameters.name: payment-product

parameters.description: A session token generated by the ASPSP to ensure that an authentication session is maintained, in order to avoid re-entering client username and password (SFA) during the execution of the SCA
parameters.name: aspsp-authentication-session-id

get.summary: This API retrieve the status of a Bulk payment initiation.

parameters.description: The Identification of the PSU in the ASPSP client interface.
parameters.name: psu-id

parameters.description: Type of the PSU-ID, needed in scenarios where PSUs have several PSU-IDs as access possibility.
parameters.name: psu-id-type

parameters.description: Identification of a Corporate, only used in a corporate context
parameters.name: psu-corporate-id

parameters.description: This is describing the type of the identification needed by the ASPSP to identify the PSU-Corporate-ID content.
parameters.name: psu-corporate-id-type

parameters.description: The forwarded Agent header field of the http request between PSU and TPP.  The User-Agent request header contains a characteristic string that allows the network protocol peers to identify the application type, operating system, software vendor or software version of the requesting software user agent.
parameters.name: psu-user-agent

parameters.description: The forwarded IP Address header field consists of the corresponding http request IP Address field between PSU and TPP.
parameters.name: psu-ip-address

parameters.description: The forwarded IP Port header field consists of the corresponding HTTP request IP Port field between PSU and TPP, if available.
parameters.name: psu-ip-port

parameters.description: The Accept request-header field can be used to specify certain media types which are acceptable for the response
parameters.name: psu-accept

parameters.description: The charset information sent by the PSU.  The Accept-Charset request-header field can be used to indicate what character sets are acceptable for the response.
parameters.name: psu-accept-charset

parameters.description: The encoding information sent by the PSU. The Accept-Encoding request-header field is similar to Accept but restricts the content-codings that are acceptable in the response.
parameters.name: psu-accept-encoding

parameters.description: The language information sent by the PSU.  The Accept-Language request-header field is similar to Accept, but restricts the set of natural languages that are preferred as a response to the request
parameters.name: psu-accept-language

parameters.description: HTTP method used at the PSU – TPP interface, if available. Valid values are: - GET - POST - PUT - PATCH - DELETE
parameters.name: psu-http-method

parameters.description: UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) for a device, which is used by the PSU, if available.UUID identifies either a device or a device dependant application installation. In case of an installation identification this ID need to be unaltered until removal from device.
parameters.name: psu-device-id

parameters.description: The forwarded Geo Location of the corresponding http request between PSU and TPP if available.Format using [RFC 2426], i.e. GEO:<latitude>,< longitude >.
parameters.name: psu-geo-location

parameters.description: A unique registration number for TPP identification
parameters.name: tpp-registration-number

parameters.description: The TPP name
parameters.name: tpp-name

parameters.description: The identification of the ASPSP (for example ABI)
parameters.name: aspsp-code

parameters.description: Identifier of the product in ASPSP scope.
parameters.name: aspsp-product-code

parameters.description: UUID of the request, unique to the call, as determined by the initiating party.
parameters.name: request-id

parameters.description: The number of bits used to display one color.The possible values are: 32, 24, 16, or 8
parameters.name: screen-color-depth

parameters.description: Width of the user's screen Note For iOS, the resolution measurement is in points. For other operating systems, the resolution measurement is in pixels.
parameters.name: screen-width

parameters.description: Height of the user’s screen  Note For iOS, the resolution measurement is in points. For other operating systems, the resolution measurement is in pixels.
parameters.name: screen-height

parameters.description: Vertical space of the user’s screen.  Note For iOS, the resolution measurement is in points. For other operating systems, the resolution measurement is in pixels.
parameters.name: screen-vertical-space

parameters.description: The software plug-ins installed in the user’s browser.
parameters.name: software-plug-ins

parameters.description: The user’s current time zone, related to UTC (for example UTC+01:00)
parameters.name: time-zone

parameters.description: The language that the user has set in the browser
parameters.name: browser-language

parameters.description: A Boolean value that indicates whether Java is enabled in the browserOnly “true” or “false” value is accepted.
parameters.name: java-support

parameters.description: A Boolean value that indicates whether cookies are enabled in the user’s browserOnly “true” or “false” value is accepted.
parameters.name: psu-cookie-support

parameters.description: The operating system installed.
parameters.name: device-os

parameters.description: The operating system version installed
parameters.name: device-os-version

parameters.description: The major version of the browser.
parameters.name: major-version

parameters.description: The browser being used. Possible values are: Netscape, Mozilla, Explorer, Camino, Firefox, Konqueror, iCab, Opera, Safari, OmniWeb, Chrome.
parameters.name: browser-type

parameters.description: Different identifiers are retrieved for each of the operating systems: -iOS 4.x-6.x The Wi-Fi MAC address. -iOS 7.x and later  The identifierFor Vendor property of the UIDevice class. -Android The Internationa l Mobile Equipment Identity (IMEI) for GSM.
parameters.name: hardware-id

parameters.description: A positive value indicates that the device is rooted or jailbroken.Only “true” or “false” value is accepted.
parameters.name: device-rooted

parameters.description: A positive value indicates that the client device is an emulator.Only “true” or “false” value is accepted.
parameters.name: emulator

parameters.description: The client application creates and saves a random unique identifier, which the client application can read. This enables verification that the application is genuine and not malicious malware. Note: For iOS system, the value is permanently stored in the keychain. For Android system the value is stored in the private storage of the application. This value is only stored until the application is uninstalled.
parameters.name: rsa-application-key

parameters.description: The currently installed RSA Adaptive Authentication Mobile SDK Modules version and build number.
parameters.name: sdk-version

parameters.description: The Date general-header field represents the date and time at which the message was originated.  Format: EEE, dd MMM yyyy hh:mm:ss z
parameters.name: Date

parameters.description: The model of the mobile device.
parameters.name: device-model

parameters.description: The mobile device name defined by the end user.  Note In Android, this device element is returned based on the name defined in the Bluetooth settings.
parameters.name: device-name

parameters.description: The language displayed in the mobile device.
parameters.name: languages

parameters.description: Indicates whether or not the mobile device supports multitasking.Only “true” or “false” value is accepted.
parameters.name: multitasking-supported

parameters.description: A GSM Cell ID (CID) is a unique number used to identify each base transceiver station (BTS) or sector of a BTS within a location area code (LAC) or GSM network.  Note This device element is not supported by iOS.
parameters.name: cell-tower-id

parameters.description: The height of the mobile device above the ellipsoid. This device element is measured in meters.
parameters.name: geolocation-altitude

parameters.description: The accuracy of the mobile device’s altitude. This device element is returned in meters.
parameters.name: geolocation-altitude-accuracy

parameters.description: The direction of travel of the mobile device. This device element is returned in degrees.
parameters.name: geolocation-heading

parameters.description: The radius of uncertainty for the geo-location of the mobile device. This device element is measured in meters. A negative value indicates that the geo-location latitude or longitude of the mobile device is invalid.
parameters.name: geolocation-horizontal-accuracy

parameters.description: The current ground speed of the mobile device. This device element is returned in meters per second.
parameters.name: geolocation-speed

parameters.description: There are five possible status codes:0=success The geolocation is successfully retrieved. 1=deny The end user denies location collection. 2=location status not available. The geolocation position is unavailable. 3=location status timeout The geolocation is timed out. 4=location status not supported The geolocation collection is not supported.
parameters.name: geolocation-status

parameters.description: The time that the geo-location was created. This device element is returned in milliseconds.
parameters.name: geolocation-timestamp

parameters.description: The local area code. Note For Android, this device element is only available for GSM devices and does not support CDMA devices. Note This device element is not supported by iOS.
parameters.name: location-area-code

parameters.description: The Wi-Fi card MAC address.  Note This device element is mandatory for iOS versions 4.x– 6.x.
parameters.name: wifi-mac-address

parameters.description: The country code of the mobile device.
parameters.name: MCC

parameters.description: The mobile carrier code.
parameters.name: MNC

parameters.description: The basic service set identification (BSSID) for each basic service set.
parameters.name: wifi-networks-data-bssid

parameters.description: The wireless signal strength in the database management system expressed in decibel/meters For example -40 dBmNote This device element is not supported by iOS.
parameters.name: wifi-networks-data-signal-strength

parameters.description: The Service Set Identifier (SSID). (the network name)
parameters.name: wifi-networks-data-ssid

parameters.description: Define the channel used from PSU in order to send the request to TPP (WEB or APP)
parameters.name: channel

parameters.description: The operating system identification:- iOS: Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) (in this case it is length 36 characters)-Android: The client application creates a random unique identifier that permanently persists in the keychain (Android_ID) (in this case it is length 16 characters)
parameters.name: os-id

parameters.description: Mobile Station International Subscriber Directory Number (MSISDN).
parameters.name: phone-number

parameters.description: The International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI)
parameters.name: sim-id

parameters.description: The addressed payment product endpoint.The value list of products supported is: - sepa-credit-transfers - instant-sepa-credit-transfers - target-2-payments - cross-border-credit-transfers
parameters.name: payment-product

parameters.description: PSD2 gateway payment identification.  The UUID of the generated payment initiation resource.
parameters.name: payment-id

parameters.description: TransactionDecisionDelegationForBulkRestRequest
parameters.name: body

delete.summary: This API allows to execute a payment cancellation requesto towards ASPSP.

ChallengeData.description: Data to generate OTP. The parameter is valorized only in the case of “EMBEDDED” SCA Approach.Object Type: Challenge Data

additionalInformation.description: Additional explanation for the PSU to explain e.g. fallback mechanism for the chosen SCA method. The TPP is obliged to show this to the PSU.

image.description: PNG data (max. 512 kilobyte) to be displayed to the PSU, Base64 encoding , cp. [RFC 4648]. This attribute is used only, when PHOTO_OTP or CHIP_OTP is the selected SCA method

imageLink.description: A link where the ASPSP will provides the challenge image for the TPP.

otpFormat.description: The format type of the OTP to be typed in. The admitted values are:charactersinteger.

otpMaxLength.description: The maximal length for the OTP to be typed in by the PSU.

paymentTransactionStatus.description: Status of the payment transaction on the bank systems. See Transaction Status

DeletePaymentRestResponse202.description: PSD2 - delete payment rest response in case of http status 202

paymentTransactionStatus.description: Status of the payment transaction on the bank systems. See Transaction Status

errorCode.description: Code that identifies the error occurred

FraudScoreLevel.description: Define for each rule level the related score (this is valorized in case the fraudResult is different to 000)

score.description: Score calculated to forehead of a violation of the rule level

GetBulkPaymentStatusRequestRestResponse.description: PSD2 Bulk - get bulk payment status request rest response

paymentTransactionStatus.description: Status of the payment transaction on the bank systems. See Transaction Status

psuMessage.description: Message to be shown to the PSU. This parameter should be used in case of multi SCA authorization. The bank will provide here the information needed to the TPP and PSU to complete the authorization of the payment.

PaymentAuthorizationPsuAuthenticationRes.description: PSD2 - payment authorization psu authentication rest request
PSD2 - payment authorization psu authentication rest response

endUserIdentification.description: End user identification cross bank. The value is derived through function of SHA512 of the concatenation of PSU fiscal code and the following string: 57Z08PPmOedjjl4YLuRauc2nMoAckyQdZG5iSfmskX2jnO8LLO0jCOUCEVl5k5V19DD8xTwgg522trK3cx02, An example:enduserIdentification=SHA512(C.F.+ 57Z08PPmOedjjl4YLuRauc2nMoAckyQdZG5iSfmskX2jnO8LLO0jCOUCEVl5k5V19DD8xTwgg522trK3cx02If it is a corporate user the concatenation is the VAT number with the following string:  njMqlpKSrwS6pObSRZgMcDVV6O2YyBujTckN1Z4gXB8FPvBQE6pYXpVZYxrckZsw4B1wCWAy7IgAi1sCtQWhzBBG7An example:enduserIdentification=SHA512(P.I. + njMqlpKSrwS6pObSRZgMcDVV6O2YyBujTckN1Z4gXB8FPvBQE6pYXpVZYxrckZsw4B1wCWAy7IgAi1sCtQWhzBBG7)

psuCorporateId.description: Identification of a Corporate, only used in a corporate context

psuCorporateIdType.description: This is describing the type of the identification needed by the ASPSP to identify the PSU-Corporate-ID content.

psuId.description: The identification of the PSU in the ASPSP client interface.

psuIdType.description: Type of the PSU-ID, needed in scenarios where PSUs have several PSU-IDs as access possibility.

psuMessage.description: Text to be displayed to the PSU.

psuName.description: Name of the PSU.In case of a corporate account, this might be the person acting on behalf of the corporate.

PaymentAuthorizationResourceScaInitiatio.description: PSD2 - payment authorization resource sca initiation rest request

authorisationCallbackUrl.description: Callback partial URL (without IP and PORT) of the PSD2, where the bank notifies the SCA result. The parameter will be valorized only in the case “Redirect” or “Decoupled” approach.

psuAlreadyAuthenticated.description: 'true', if the psu has been already authenticated through the platform; 'false', otherwise.

selectedScaApproach.description: The SCA approach selected. Possible values are: EMBEDDED DECOUPLED REDIRECT OAUTH

PaymentAuthorizationResourceScaInitiatio.description: PSD2 - payment authorization resource sca initiation rest response

aspspScaUrl.description: URL to which the TPP must redirect the PSU to perform the SCA (it is valorized only in the case oAuth or Redirect SCA).

psuMessage.description: Text to be displayed to the PSU.In the case of the Decoupled SCA approach this field will be valorized with PSU Instructions to execute.

PaymentAuthorizationScaValidationRequest.description: PSD2 - payment authorization sca validation request rest request

scaAuthenticationData.description: SCA authentication data, depending on the chosen authentication method. If the data is binary, then it is base64 encoded.

PaymentAuthorizationScaValidationRequest.description: PSD2 - payment authorization sca validation request rest response

paymentTransactionStatus.description: Status of the payment transaction. See Transaction Status

psuMessage.description: Text to be displayed to the PSU

PsuCredential.description: PSU Credentials on the Bank systemObject Type: PSU Credential

productCode.description: Bank Product to which the user must authenticate.

RestHref.description: The link to the authorisation end-point, where the cancellation-authorisation sub-resource has to be generated while selecting the authentication method. This link is contained under exactly the same conditions as the data element 'scaMethods”.This link can be used in the following scenarios:The PSU ID provided during the cancellation isn’t the same that authorized the payment. The platform identifys more SCA methods applicable, so the PSU has to choise one of them to starts an explicit authorisationThe payment was SCA exempt and there are more SCA methods applicable, so the PSU has to choise one of them

ScaMethod.description: The SCA Method selectedObject Type: Sca Method

authenticationMethodId.description: An identification provided by the ASPSP for the later identification of the authentication method selection.

authenticationType.description: The field describes the type of the authentication method.Example values are:SMS_OTPCHIP_OTPPHOTO_OTPPUSH_OTP

authenticationVersion.description: Depending on the “authenticationType”. This version can be used by differentiating authentication tools used within performing OTP generation in the same authentication type. This version can be referred to in the ASPSP’s documentation.

explanation.description: This is a description about the authentication method.

name.description: This could be a description provided by the ASPSP like “SMS OTP on phone +49160 xxxxx 28”.This name shall be used by the TPP when presenting a list of authentication methods to the PSU, if available.

SignatureData.description: ASPSP signature. Object Type: Signature Data

aspspCertificate.description: The APSPS certificate used to verify the signature

digest.description: The field is hash concatenation of the parameters which represents the payment. It could include the following parameters (to be defined with banks) paymentIdpaymentTransactionStatusDate (one received in input header)All fields are separated by pipe eg:SHA512(paymentId|paymentTransactionStatus|Date)

digitalSignature.description: The signature of the HASH by the ASPSP through its own encryption key

TransactionDecisionData.description: Details on analysis performed from ASPSP systemObject Type: Transaction Decision Data

currencyConversionFees.description: Might be used by the ASPSP to transport specific currency conversion fees related to the initiated credit transfer.

debtorName.description: This field must contain the “owner name” info of the debited account. The “owner name” is the name of the legal account owner. If there is more than one owner, then e.g. two names might be noted here.For a corporate account, the corporate name is used for this attribute. This enables the ASPSP to transport the account owner name to the PISP in case where the regulatory need is provided and if not provided by other means like the List of Available Accounts Service.

estimatedInterbankSettlementAmount.description: The estimated amount to be transferred to the payee.

estimatedTotalAmount.description: The amount which is estimated to be debted from the debtor account.Note: This amount includes fees.

fraudResult.description: Overall outcome assigned by the ASPSP system.The transaction is granted if the value is between 000 – 099 while will be denied (and consequently the flow interrupted) if the value is >= 100 (100 included)

fraudScoreLevel.description: Define for each rule level the related score (this is valorized in case the fraudResult is different to 000)

psuMessage.description: If the consumer is fraudster, the filed will be populated the specific error message

transactionFeeIndicator.description: If equals “true”, the transaction will involve specific transaction cost as shown by the ASPSP in their public price list or as agreed between ASPSP and PSU. If equals 'false', the transaction will not involve additional specific transaction costs to the PSU.

TransactionDecisionDelegationForBulkRest.description: PSD2 - transaction decision delegation for bulk rest response

TransactionFee.description: Can be used by the ASPSP to returns transaction fees relevant for the underlying payments.

amount.description: The amount given with fractional digits, where fractions must be compliant to the currency definition. Up to 14 significant figures. Negative amounts are signed by minus. The decimal separator is a dot. Example: Valid representations for EUR with up to two decimals are: • 1056 • 5768.2 • -1.50• 5877.78

currency.description: Define the code of a currency, three letters for the country. (CurrencyCode ISO 20022)
